one_time_description: Province of British Columbia revenue of $20,731,029 in 2023 vs $3,650,377 prior year — likely large one-time provincial transfer; also earnings from investment in Nation business entities of $9,646,741
economic_notes: Four business entities accounted for using modified equity method: Halfway River Holdings Ltd., Halfway River Holdings Limited Partnership, Halfway River Group Ltd. (incorporated March 20, 2023), and Six Nations Ventures Limited Partnership (16.67% interest). Halfway River Holdings Limited Partnership owes First Nation a $18,487,387 note payable. First Nation has a specific claim filed in BC Supreme Court for Treaty 8 rights infringement with negotiations ongoing.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 — auditor's report dated February 11, 2025, which is unusually late (nearly 2 years after year-end). Four entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs), not consolidated. Province of BC revenue spiked to $20.7M from $3.65M prior year — likely large one-time transfer. Tangible capital assets net book value not fully extracted as the note was cut off in the source text. Promissory notes payable of $3,323,685 ($2,927,071 + $396,614) related to Treaty 8 specific claims advance from ISC not included in long-term debt figure above. Funds held in Ottawa Trust of $3,641,685 recorded on balance sheet. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text.
